main_sections.title: The Big Laddoo
main_sections.poem_text:
If all the laddoos were one Laddoo
Oh! What a BIG Laddoo it would be!

If all the children were one child
Oh! What a BIG child that would be!

If all the seas were one sea
Oh! What a BIG sea it would be!

Then if the BIG child
picked the BIG Laddoo
And threw it into the BIG sea
Oh! What a BIG SPLISH-SPLASH it would be!
main_sections.poem_appreciation_summary: The poem is a cumulative, playful fantasy built on the imaginative premise of collapsing all members of a category — laddoos, children, seas — into one impossibly giant version. The tone is exuberant and wonder-filled. The dominant structural device is anaphora ('If all the … were one …') which builds rhythmic momentum across three stanzas before releasing into a gleeful final action: the giant child hurling the giant laddoo into the giant sea. The central image is joyful gigantism. The onomatopoeic 'SPLISH-SPLASH' is the climactic device, landing with comic delight. Theme: the pleasure of boundless imagination and playful 'what if' thinking.

tasks_verbatim.task_text: Let us recite the poem 'The Big Laddoo'.

oracy.section_name: Let us think + Let us speak

tasks_verbatim.task_text:
Let us think.
1. Have you seen a big laddoo? How big was it? Did you eat it all by yourself? Tell your friends about it.
2. Have you eaten anything that is very big? What was it? Talk about it.
3. What different kinds of laddoos have you eaten?

question_bank.stem: Have you seen a big laddoo? How big was it? Did you eat it all by yourself? Tell your friends about it.
Have you eaten anything that is very big? What was it? Talk about it.
What different kinds of laddoos have you eaten?

tasks_verbatim.task_text:
Let us speak.
Jithu and Meena are friends. Meena cannot see properly. It was her birthday. Jithu gifted her a box of mixed sweets. She felt the sweets and excitedly picked up the big laddoo. She said, 'I love laddoos!' Jithu was surprised and asked her, 'How did you know it was a laddoo?' Think and say what Meena answered.

question_bank.stem: Think and say what Meena answered when Jithu asked: 'How did you know it was a laddoo?'

visual_stimulus.brief_description: Jithu and Meena (with glasses) at a birthday table, Meena holding a laddoo from a box of mixed sweets, balloons and cake in background

writing.section_name: Let us think + Let us write

tasks_verbatim.task_text:
Let us think.
4. Name a few things that are round in shape. Can you eat all of them? Write them in the correct column.
That can be eaten | That cannot be eaten

question_bank.stem: Name a few things that are round in shape. Write them in the correct column: 'That can be eaten' and 'That cannot be eaten'.
question_bank.table:
That can be eaten|That cannot be eaten
(student response)|(student response)

tasks_verbatim.task_text:
Let us think.
5. The child threw the Big Laddoo into the sea. What do you think happened to the Laddoo?

question_bank.stem: The child threw the Big Laddoo into the sea. What do you think happened to the Laddoo?

tasks_verbatim.task_text:
Let us write.
A. Frame a few sentences using the given words.
1. play
2. plum
3. cloud
4. clock
5. blue
6. blanket

question_bank.stem: Frame a sentence using the word: play
Frame a sentence using the word: plum
Frame a sentence using the word: cloud
Frame a sentence using the word: clock
Frame a sentence using the word: blue
Frame a sentence using the word: blanket

tasks_verbatim.task_text:
Let us write.
A. Which is your favourite festival? Write the names of some sweets and dishes prepared on that day.

question_bank.stem: Which is your favourite festival? Write the names of some sweets and dishes prepared on that day.

word_work.section_name: Let us learn + Let us write

tasks_verbatim.task_text:
Let us learn.
Laddoos are sweets, commonly eaten in every house. Write words that tell us about the laddoo.

question_bank.stem: Write words that tell us about the laddoo (describing words / adjectives).

tasks_verbatim.task_text:
Let us write.
B. Match the following.
1. cock — lioness / cow / hen / duck / peahen / horse
2. lion
3. peacock
4. mare
5. bull
6. drake
(Match male animals to their female counterparts.)

question_bank.stem: Match the male animals to their female counterparts: cock, lion, peacock, mare, bull, drake — with lioness, cow, hen, duck, peahen, horse.

tasks_verbatim.task_text:
Let us learn.
Did you know that not only sweets but a few other dishes are also prepared especially during festivals? [Informational text about khichdi, Sankranti festival, and regional names — Pongal, Lohri, Bihu, Maghi Sankrant, Makara Sankranti, Sakraat, Magha Saaji. Khichdi known by different names: Gujarati khichdi (Gujarat), Pongal (Tamil Nadu), Bihari khichdi (Bihar), Bajra khichdi (Rajasthan), Kichuri (West Bengal), Bisi bele bhat (Karnataka), Moong khetchir (Kashmir).]

A. Look at these objects shown here. Collect and put them one by one into a tub of water. See which one will float and which one will sink. Tick the objects that float and cross the objects that sink.
a piece of paper
a small stone
a green leaf
a pencil
an eraser
a feather
a spoon

question_bank.stem: Collect each object listed (paper, stone, leaf, pencil, eraser, feather, spoon) and test in a tub of water. Tick objects that float and cross objects that sink.

visual_stimulus.brief_description: Seven small illustrations of objects — piece of paper, small stone, green leaf, pencil, eraser, feather, spoon — shown beside blank lines for tick/cross results, with an illustration of a child in a water tub
